name: Mazanderani
notes: Mazanderani (مازِرونی) — Northwestern Iranian (Caspian) language of northern Iran. Written in the Perso-Arabic (Arabic) script, with the Persian alphabet extended for Mazanderani vowels. GRAPHEME INVENTORY NOT YET SPECIFIED: as an abjad, short vowels are normally unwritten (optional harakat aside), so grapheme→phoneme conversion requires a lexicon or vowel-restoration model rather than a letter table. INPUT CONTRACT: input is expected in the Perso-Arabic Mazanderani orthography. PHONEME INVENTORY: attested inventory from PHOIBLE (glottocode maza1291), declared directly. `graphemes` stays empty because none of that depends on the writing system: the sounds of this language are known regardless of whether — or how — it is written.

sources.author: Moran, Steven & McCloy, Daniel (eds.)
sources.title: PHOIBLE 2.0
sources.publisher: Max Planck Institute for the Science of Human History
sources.notes: Attested phoneme inventory for glottocode maza1291.
